item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The image that was used in this article has been replaced with the following image, which is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0):

Source: SCP Foundation Wiki
License: CC BY-SA 3.0
Title: 1796-new.png
Author: Elogee FishTruck
Release year: 2020

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could you be more specific as to what you feel works and doesn't work?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's nice, but lacking. The idea is great, but the execution leaves a lot to be desired; you just don't do enough with the material so it flops. I can't really say exactly why, so I would recommend a full rewrite just to be sure.

Downvoted

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Reading over the comments, I big draw for this article is how well the UIU shines in it. I don't want to draw attention away from that, since UIUseless is a trope I'm happy to see go by the wayside, and think the focus should remain on the UIU. Not one to reinvent the wheel, a simple link to do just that would be a UIU hub link. As such, I am recommending the following edit:

Changing this:

SCP-1796 was contained after being discovered and documented by the Unusual Incidents Unit. Prior to containment by the Foundation, UIU operatives had discovered the nature of SCP-1796's effect, which was documented during SCP-1796's containment operations. Foundation operatives have found the UIU's documentation of E-1765 to be inaccurate, and have archived it in Site-77. It has been classified as an anomalous object. Information collected by the UIU has been determined accurate, and is currently being used in its containment.

To this:

SCP-1796 was contained after being discovered and documented by the Unusual Incidents Unit. Prior to containment by the Foundation, UIU operatives had discovered the nature of SCP-1796's effect, which was documented during SCP-1796's containment operations. Foundation operatives have found the UIU's documentation of E-1765 to be inaccurate, and have archived it in Site-77. It has been classified as an anomalous object. Information collected by the UIU has been determined accurate, and is currently being used in its containment.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The fact that SCP-1976 seems to call for its own continued use in the test log is a great touch. It is also interesting to consider that the information being obtained by SCP-1976 may be going somewhere else. Spooky indeed.

That being said, documents that have the "don't trust it because it alters itself" thing going on are always a frustrating read for me, because I hate second guessing myself through what is otherwise an enjoyable read. Neutral vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Got me with that last documentation

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get it, but maybe is because I am tired and English is my second language I will reread it tomorrow

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe that is SCP-1796 doing the thing where it alters documents that go through it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lonely boy section feels really out of place and non-clinical, though I feel like it was intentional. I just can't figure out what it means. Neutral vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm with tapnek, what mistakes? Is it sending the information somewhere? It only makes the info visible if you touch so, so no big deal, right? What's the lonely boy doing there?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one made me feel… sad. I'm not sure why.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't get why they would have a lonely boy test out scp-1796. I'm guessing it's to prevent the Foundation from repeating the UIU's mistakes.

Edit: Oh shit, I get it now. Nice use of meta.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Waitwaitwait.
So are the changes to the documents permanent if they're "fetched" from elsewhere and altered?
Because it looks like this thing has tweaked it's own test log or something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really don't care for this item. It's boring.

But I upvoted without regrets for the UIU addenda, which is the first UIU-related material on the site that I can recall (thoroughly) liking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No personnel who have authored containment documentation are to interact with SCP-1796,

I think this is too narrow. A junior researcher might have recorded sensitive information without having ever have edited any containment documentation. An agent or MTF member who never edited any containment documentation might have written an after-action report containing sensitive information.

When a subject comes into contact with SCP-1796, a document will be transported into it.

So is information that has only been recorded as a computer file safe, or will a printed copy manifest? If the only copies ever made were shredded, will they be put back together?

The document will contain content the subject wishes to suppress knowledge of, such as classified documents, personal communications, and information the subject would be punished for owning or revealing.

Seems like this would be super-useful to the Foundation as a something similar to an interrogation device.

If the subject removes the document, a different one will appear in its place.

What if someone else tries to remove it? Does the document disappear? Does it come out, but it's not replaced by a new one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a lot of "huh?" about this.
I like it, but I'm not sure I get it. What is going on with the last line of the table? Is the envelope supposed to be altering things that it didn't teleport?

Still, it's well done, and giving the UIU credit for not being utterly clueless is worth extra points, so +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I hadn't seen that, but it actually makes sense. The UIU is run by the USA government, right? It stands to reason that they have standing orders to aid the Foundation, or at least any Foundation liaison.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have something in the work that uses them. Possibly; it's a little unfinished.

This is pretty good, although I feel the Foundation would have cleaned up the test log a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's rare to see the UIU get a mention.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the fact that this is a UIU SCP. I'm not sure I quite get all the implications, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I… really feel like I'm missing something here. I read the note and the SCP twice but I really don't feel like I get it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is the first one, if you don't count Scantron's 001 proposal

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That last test log…

I really like this. It stands on its own, and then it turns into a good UIU SCP. (Do we even have any of those?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fuck I didn't even notice that one…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The year 1976 just keeps turning up…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't mind the idea, but this seems a little too inconspicuous to be an SCP. I mean, an envelope that shows you what you don't want found, ok. the continuous use properties seem like they could be elaborated on, as that seems somewhat cool, but unless it has some interesting stuff happen after continued use, more interesting than what's currently there, it seems just anomalous to me..although, I give you credit for bringing up the UIU, as I've seen very little actual stuff related to them, but other than that….meh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm feeling nothing, no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the concept in and of itself, and the note as Troy said. What really makes me like that note, though, isn't just that they know about the Foundation, but that this implies that they know full well that even though they found this, it is going to be taken. So they have resigned themselves to that fact, and they are at least trying to make a difference by passing on the knowledge they've gleaned about it, so the Foundation can have an easier time containing it. Not that the Foundation necessarily needed it, but this shows that at least one UIU agent still has hope of making a difference.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not digging the actual object that much, as it feels a lot like a replication of Tanhony's style. On the other hand, I really, really like the little UIU note at the bottom, and I think the fact that someone there actually seems to know both about the Foundation and what they do is awesome. Neutral voted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. Don't love it though. I can't put my finger on it, but all your scips share the same problem, that something just seems off or is missing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very good use of meta. I like it.
